student_need: If you are experiencing an emergency , a life-or limb-threatening situations or immediate safety concerns, call 911 or Go to the nearest hospital emergency department. You may also consult the Student Wellness Hub’s urgent care page to find an appropriate resource. Anyone living in Québec can also call Info-Santé, a free confidential telephone consultation service, at 811 to speak to a nurse 24/7.
intended_users: International students seeking guidance on how to access healthcare in Quebec.
access_method: Local Wellness Advisors: https://www.mcgill.ca/wellness-hub/about-hub/clinicians/local-wellness-advisors | book appointments: https://www.mcgill.ca/wellness-hub/get-support | 514-398-7992: tel:514-398-7992 | 514-329-1999: tel:514-329-1999
recommended_next_step: Review the appropriate care route; for emergencies call 911 or go to the nearest emergency department.
limitations: The navigator must not diagnose, recommend treatment, or determine whether symptoms are an emergency.

student_need: The Career Planning Service (CaPS), McGill’s central career services, supports student career and leadership development through career counselling and advising, skills development workshops, experiential learning, career exploration programs, mentoring, and career and networking events. CaPS provides and oversees a wide variety of resources and tools to help students search for part-time and summer jobs, internships and post-graduation opportunities, including the myFuture platform, and the Student Experience Record , with the goal of helping students become career-ready now and for their future.
intended_users: McGill students seeking career advising, skills development, jobs, internships, and networking resources.
recommended_next_step: Review CaPS services and use the official advising route when personalized career support is needed.
limitations: Career support does not determine work authorization; international students should confirm permit rules separately.

recommended_next_step: Review Campus Life and Engagement programs to identify a suitable involvement or connection opportunity.
limitations: Program schedules, availability, and intended audiences should be confirmed on official program pages.

student_need: There are many resources available to students in the broader health and social services community in Montreal and Quebec. Some are at no-cost, but many do have out-of-pocket fees or require you to submit claims to your health insurance. The information below may be subject to change. Please check with the resource for more details on eligibility, services, and/or costs.
intended_users: McGill students seeking health or social-service resources in Montreal and Quebec.
access_method: Get support from our team.: https://www.mcgill.ca/wellness-hub/get-support/mental-health-support/find-mental-health-appointment | Tel : 514 285-5500: tel:514-285-5500 | Montreal Abortion Appointment Center: https://santemontreal.qc.ca/en/public/support-and-services/abortion-montreal-appointment-centre/ | 514 345-1356: tel:1-514-345-1356
recommended_next_step: Review the relevant resource category and confirm services, eligibility, and costs with the provider.
limitations: Listings may change and may involve fees or insurance claims; inclusion does not guarantee suitability.

student_need: McGill recognises the challenges International students can face when studying away from home. Scholarships and Student Aid is committed to ensuring that qualified students from any geographic region are financially supported in their goals to enter and complete academic programs at the University. This page points to the various funding opportunities that will help support these goals. Newly admitted undergraduate students can apply to McGill’s Entrance Scholarship Program . In-course award programs for undergraduate students currently studying at McGill fall under individual faculty jurisdiction as do those for graduate students .
intended_users: International students exploring McGill scholarships, awards, financial aid, and related funding.
recommended_next_step: Review the funding categories and follow the official application route for the relevant program.
limitations: Funding eligibility and application requirements vary by program and must be confirmed officially.

student_need: McGill's Off-Campus Housing resource offers tools to help students find housing near the downtown and Macdonald campuses, including a partnership with Places4Students for rental searches, student sublets, and roommate profiles.
intended_users: McGill students looking for apartments, sublets, or roommates off campus, including newcomers arriving in Montreal.
access_method: Rental Information: https://www.mcgill.ca/students/housing/offcampus/rental-information | Apartment Listings: https://www.mcgill.ca/students/housing/offcampus/listings | Useful Information: https://www.mcgill.ca/students/housing/offcampus/useful-information

limitations: The navigator does not vet landlords, listings, or lease terms and does not provide legal housing or tenant-rights advice; verify all rental details independently.

student_need: Service Point is McGill's central student services hub that assists students with administrative needs such as ID cards, transcripts, enrolment and verification documents, tuition matters, and course-related requests.
intended_users: McGill students who need help with ID cards, transcripts, enrolment and verification documents, tuition matters, and course-related administrative requests.
access_method: Home: https://www.mcgill.ca/servicepoint/ | Contact Us: https://www.mcgill.ca/servicepoint/contact | Student Services & Resources: https://www.mcgill.ca/students/directory | 514-398-7878: tel:514-398-7878
recommended_next_step: Identify the document or administrative request you need and use the official Service Point page or contact details to proceed.
limitations: Administrative requirements, fees, deadlines, and processing times can change; confirm details on the official Service Point page or with Service Point staff before acting.

student_need: McGill Student Accounts manages student finances including tuition and fees, e-billing, and payments, and provides the information students need to manage the financial-account side of starting at McGill.
intended_users: McGill students (and their sponsors) who need information about tuition, fees, e-billing, and payments.
access_method: Tuition & fees: https://www.mcgill.ca/student-accounts/tuition-fees | e-Billing & your account: https://www.mcgill.ca/student-accounts/your-account | Make a payment: https://www.mcgill.ca/student-accounts/your-account/payment | New students: https://www.mcgill.ca/student-accounts/new-students/general
recommended_next_step: Review the tuition, e-billing, and payment pages for your situation and follow the official payment instructions and deadlines.
limitations: Fee amounts, deadlines, and accepted payment methods change each term; confirm current amounts and due dates on the official Student Accounts pages before paying.

student_need: Find all the resources and advice you need to help pay for university, from step-by-step guides on how to apply for funding to information on how to budget effectively. Not sure where to start? These areas are a good bet (or you can click on our full directory drop-down list below ): Work on campus or in a McGill-affiliated hospital/organization

service_name: International Student Work and Permits Guidance
student_need: As a full-time International McGill student, you can work both on campus and off-campus, but it's important to ensure that you have the right documents to do so legally. “ Work ” is defined in the Immigration Canada Regulations as an activity for which wages are paid or commission is earned, OR that competes directly with activities of Canadian citizens or permanent residents in the Canadian labour market.
intended_users: Full-time international McGill students seeking official guidance about working during their studies.
recommended_next_step: Review the applicable work topic and confirm that the required documents and authorization are in place.
limitations: The navigator must not interpret permit conditions or determine whether a student is legally authorized to work.
source_name: International Student Work and Permits Guidance
